questions.question: State the temperature, in K, and pressure, in kPa, that define RTP (room temperature and pressure) in OCR A Chemistry.
Calculate the volume, in cm³, occupied by 0.125 mol of oxygen gas (O₂) at RTP.
A sample of propane gas (C₃H₈, M = 44.1 g mol⁻¹) has a volume of 480 cm³ at 27 °C and 150 kPa. Calculate the mass of propane in this sample. [R = 8.314 J mol⁻¹ K⁻¹]
State the value of the gas constant R, including its unit.
Calculate the amount, in mol, of hydrogen gas (H₂) in 4.80 dm³ at RTP.

A rigid container holds 2.00 g of argon gas (Ar, M = 39.9 g mol⁻¹) at a temperature of 57 °C. The volume of the container is 1.50 dm³. Calculate the pressure, in kPa, inside the container. [R = 8.314 J mol⁻¹ K⁻¹]
State the units in which pressure (p), volume (V), and temperature (T) must be expressed when using the equation pV = nRT.
Calculate the molar mass, in g mol⁻¹, of a gas if 3.00 g occupies 2000 cm³ at RTP.

A balloon contains 0.400 mol of helium gas (He) at 25 °C and 100 kPa. (i) Calculate the volume of the balloon in dm³ using pV = nRT. (ii) Hence confirm this is consistent with the molar gas volume of 24 dm³ mol⁻¹ at RTP. [R = 8.314 J mol⁻¹ K⁻¹]
State what must be done to a temperature given in °C before it can be used in the equation pV = nRT.
Calculate the mass of 600 cm³ of chlorine gas (Cl₂, M = 71.0 g mol⁻¹) at RTP.
A student uses pV = nRT and obtains an answer 1000 times smaller than expected. Explain the most likely error the student has made, and state the correct procedure for avoiding it.
questions.context: The student used p = 101 kPa, V = 2 dm³, T = 298 K.

questions.question: State the conversion factor between dm³ and m³.
A gas sample at 37 °C and 200 kPa has a volume of 300 cm³. Calculate the amount of gas, in mol. [R = 8.314 J mol⁻¹ K⁻¹]
0.250 mol of a gas X is held at 127 °C in a 4.15 dm³ container. (i) Calculate the pressure, in Pa, of the gas. (ii) Convert this pressure to kPa. [R = 8.314 J mol⁻¹ K⁻¹]
